solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def minSteps(self, n: int) -> int:
        i = 2
        res = 0
        while i * i <= n:
            while n % i == 0:
                res += i
                n //= i
            i += 1
        if n > 1:
            res += n
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a prime factorization approach to find the minimum number of operations. It iterates from 2 to sqrt(n) and for each prime factor i, it adds i to the result and divides n by i until n is no longer divisible by i. Finally, if n is greater than 1, it adds n to the result. This approach is efficient because it only needs to iterate up to sqrt(n) and uses a constant amount of space.
